Details: Ultraviolet irradition with hydrogen peroxide can achieve complete degradation of carbofuran. Temperature and the initial carbofuran concentration were demonstrated to influence degradation by UV irradiation with hydrogen peroxide.  Increases in temperature and/or the carbofuran concentration were both demonstrated to result in increased degradation.  Ferric ion can also be used as a catalyst to accelerate the oxidation reaction (Fenton's reaction).
